issues.detail: translation is broken/corrupt: shows 'Und hacke dir ab die Krallen.' as translation instead of actual English. The text itself also appears truncated (starts with a quote but no closing). This example needs full retranslation.

Wesen gloss_hint 'personality' is wrong for this context — should be 'being' or 'creature' (Wesen = beings, not personality). vermittelt gloss_hint 'mediate' is wrong — should be 'impart' or 'convey' (vermitteln in sense of communicating values).

Multiple issues: (1) 'mich' annotated as pos:noun with lemma 'ich' and gloss_hint 'we' — should be pronoun, not noun, and 'we' is wrong (it's 'me'). (2) 'ich' annotated as pos:noun with gloss_hint 'we' — same issue. (3) verleitete gloss_hint 'led me' is acceptable but not a substring match. (4) ertragen gloss_hint 'could not bear' is too long for a hint.
laut annotated as adjective with gloss_hint 'loud | noisy | fast' — in this context 'laut' is a preposition meaning 'according to', not the adjective 'loud'. Wrong POS and wrong gloss_hint.
erwiesen gloss_hint null is OK but erweisen may need disambiguation. Prozeß lemma 'Prozess' is fine (spelling modernization).
anstellen gloss_hint 'start up' is wrong — this example uses sense 0 'lean against', not sense 6 'start up/turn on'. Should be 'lean' or null.
Ich/ich annotated as pronoun with gloss_hint 'ego' — 'ego' is not a good gloss_hint for 'ich' (the pronoun 'I'). Should be null or 'I'.

stellte lemma 'stellen' — should be 'anstellen' since this is a separable verb construction 'stellte...an'. die annotated as pronoun with gloss_hint 'which' but in context 'die Familien' it's a determiner.

Translation is awkward: 'what the doctors did to him with everything before Grandpa's death' — word order is off. Should be 'When you consider what the doctors still did to Grandpa before his death...'

Angestellte annotated with gloss_hint 'what was done' — 'Angestellte' here is nominalized 'das Angestellte' (the thing done), not the noun 'employee', so this is a rare/unusual usage. The annotation lemma 'Angestellte' is misleading.
ganz gloss_hint 'just' is misleading — here 'ganz schön' means 'quite/rather', not 'just'. schön gloss_hint 'pleasant' also misses the intensifier usage.

Neue gloss_hint 'snow (fresh)' is completely wrong — der Neue means 'the new guy/newcomer'. stellt lemma 'stellen' should be 'anstellen' (separable verb). gar gloss_hint 'done' is wrong — here 'gar nicht' means 'not at all', not 'cooked/done'.

Können gloss_hint 'manage' is wrong — here it means 'can'. Sie annotated as noun with gloss_hint 'she (pronoun)' — should be pronoun (formal you), not noun. Schalter gloss_hint 'switch' is wrong sense — here it means 'counter/window' (service counter).
anzustellen lemma 'ankündigen' is completely wrong — should be 'anstellen'. This is a serious lemma error.
Translation is incomplete — only translates the first sentence ('Phew, no one can stand that smell!') but misses the second part: 'Oh come on, don't make such a fuss!'
Translation is incomplete — only covers the response part ('Oh, come on, he's just pretending!') but misses the first sentence: 'That was a really bad foul!'
Translation says 'Remove the herbs from the carrots' but Kraut here means 'greens' (inedible tops), not 'herbs'. Should be 'Remove the greens/tops from the carrots!'

All annotations use English forms instead of German forms (e.g. form:'preparing' lemma:'vorbereiten', form:'must' lemma:'müssen'). These are annotations of the English translation text, not the German source. Completely invalid.

Translation says 'spread the herb on the bread' but Kraut here means 'molasses/syrup' (sense 7, Northwestern Germany). Should be 'You can also spread the syrup/molasses on the bread.' Also: 'das' lemma is 'which' (English) instead of German determiner.
Translation 'Your slit is open' misses the idiomatic meaning — 'Schlitz' here is sense 1 (fly/zipper). Should be 'Your fly is open.' Schlitz gloss_hint 'slot' is wrong sense.

Translation 'At the slot, you see that it is a girl' misses the vulgar meaning — 'Schlitz' here is sense 3 (vulva, vulgar). Should convey the anatomical meaning. Schlitz gloss_hint 'slot' is wrong sense.

Ich annotated as pos:noun with gloss_hint 'we' — should be pronoun, not noun, and gloss_hint should be null or 'I'. Speck annotated with lemma 'Speck' pos:noun gloss_hint 'body fat' — but Speck here is a place name, should be proper noun. geboren annotated as adjective — acceptable.

Ich annotated as pos:noun with gloss_hint 'we' — should be pronoun. Speck gloss_hint 'body fat' wrong — this is a place name. verbracht gloss_hint 'vacation' is wrong — should be null or 'spend (time)'.

All annotations use English forms instead of German (form:'mind', form:'cannot', form:'comprehend', form:'some', form:'things'). These are annotations of the English translation, not the German source text. Completely invalid.

Translation says 'The mayor has moved again' but OB in this military context (Gumrak = Stalingrad battle) means Oberbefehlshaber (commander-in-chief), not Oberbürgermeister (mayor). Should be 'The commander-in-chief has moved again, into a dugout behind Gumrak.'

Translation says 'Pass the order on to the mayor!' but in this military context OB likely means Oberbootsmann (sense 2, boatswain's mate) per the word file assignment. Should be 'Pass the order on to the senior boatswain!' or similar.
gloss_en is null for all 19 senses. This word has no short English glosses at all, only gloss_en_full. Not flagging each individually but noting the word needs gloss_en population.

Sense 0 gloss is 'Plural 1:' — this is an incomplete/structural marker, not a real definition. gloss_en 'mind' and gloss_en_full is a refusal message. This sense should be merged with sense 1 or removed.
Sense 7 gloss is 'Plural 2:' — this is an incomplete/structural marker, not a real definition. gloss_en_full is a refusal message. This sense should be merged with sense 5/6 or removed.
gloss_en 'ghost' for 'göttliches Wesen, übernatürliches Wesen' is too narrow — should be 'spirit' (divine/supernatural being). 'Ghost' implies dead person's soul which is sense 6.
synonyms_en ['stylus pen', 'writing stylus', 'digital pen'] are wrong for this sense (metal/wood pin/peg). Should be related to pin/peg, not digital pen.

Translation is awkward/wrong: 'When you consider what the doctors did to him with everything before Grandpa's death...' — should be 'When you think of everything the doctors still put him through before grandpa's death...'
gender_rule says suffix_ist matches but 'Geist' does not end in -ist. The -ist suffix rule is for words like Tourist, Polizist. Geist ends in -eist which is not the -ist suffix.
